question: Can a fixed three-perpetual monthly 90-day time-series momentum portfolio at 0.5x gross remain profitable after actual funding and costs through development, independent evaluation and confirmation while bounding drawdown and intraday adverse risk?
development_gate: data PASS; 90d base/stress total >0; 2021 and 2022 each >0; max drawdown >-35% and below continuous 0.5x long; worst daily intraday adverse >-20%; 60d and 120d base totals >0; turnover <=30
evaluation_gate: 90d base/stress total >0; 2023 and 2024 each >0; max drawdown >-35% and below continuous 0.5x long; adverse >-20%; 60d and 120d base totals >0
confirmation_gate: 90d base/stress total >0; max drawdown >-35% and below continuous 0.5x long; adverse >-20%; 60d and 120d base totals >=0
holdout_release_rule: Fetch evaluation only after development passes; fetch confirmation only after fixed evaluation passes; never change economic rules or gates after release
known_exposure: No AVAXUSDT, DOTUSDT or NEARUSDT futures Kline, funding or result data was read by prior research before this checkpoint
allowed_fixes: fill only archive-missing daily timestamps from official public USD-M Kline REST
implementation defects that do not change symbols, signals, gross, periods, costs, benchmark or gates
study_code_revision_reason: Data-boundary-only fixes after fetch stopped before analysis: skip only leading 404 archive months before a contract's first successful official month; do not treat timestamps before its first official bar as missing; record prelisting months/days. Any later 404 remains fatal. Economic rules, periods, costs and gates are unchanged.
